treeCanopy.treesPlanted2025Note: 52 trees of native species planted across three OSR-coordinated events in 2025, in areas identified by the equity prioritization tool.
treeCanopy.assessmentNote: 2025 assessment was the first comprehensive tree canopy assessment since 2009. Canopy has been tracked back to 1984 per the hub site.

telematicsPilot.comparisonContext: Data will be compared against telematics from ~300 additional City vehicles already fitted with devices from at least three other manufacturers, to select a single telematics supplier fleet-wide.
possibleFollowOnActions: Idling reduction training required for all authorized City fleet users
Identifying initial candidates for replacement with hybrid or electric vehicles
Developing a strategy to expand telematic deployment to the full City fleet

trashBash2025.format: 4 teams of local artists competing to create art installations from repurposed materials

heatActionPlanning.note: OSR participated in the state-led Planning for Extreme Heat Cohort (NC Heat Action Plan Toolkit), leading to an interlocal taskforce with Guilford County and the City of High Point that completed a heat action plan and a county-wide extreme temperatures action plan including cold-weather preparedness.

leedForCities.note: Greensboro was an inaugural city in USGBC's LEED for Cities (L4C) Certification Program (2019), earning LEED for Cities Silver Certification. The 2022-2024 GHG inventory contracted in Dec. 2025 is a component of a recertification effort.

populationAndScaleContextFromSep2022.note: Between 2007 and 2019, the City's population grew ~17%, land area increased, employee count grew, and EV utilization increased — cited context for why a ~1% drop in electricity consumption over that period is treated as meaningful in the SEP's own framing.
